title: Cantharospermum scarabaeoideum
intro: There is insufficient information available about the plant Cantharospermum scarabaeoideum.
taxonomy: Cantharospermum scarabaeoideum is a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It is native to tropical Africa. The plant's classification places it in the kingdom Plantae, phylum Angiospermophyta, class Magnoliopsida, order Sapindales, family Sapindaceae, genus Cantharospermum, and species C. scarabaeoideum. The Sapindaceae family, also known as the soapberry or soapbush family, is a group of flowering plants that are found in tropical and subtropical regions. This family includes over 1,500 species, many of which are economically important for their fruits, timber, and medicinal properties.
